---
title: "Weizheng Bai"
image: C:\Users\16526\Desktop\Weizheng Bai_2024.jpg
about:
  id: hero-heading
  template: trestles
  image-width: 12em
  image-shape: round
  links:
    - icon: mailbox
      text: baiweizheng22@mails.ucas.ac.cn
      href: mailto:baiweizheng22@mails.ucas.ac.cn
    - icon: github
      text: arlionn
      href: https://github.com/lianyujun
    - icon: book
      text: Google Scholar
      href: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=vqyiqNgAAAAJ&hl=zh-CN&oi=ao
    - icon: fingerprint
      text: ORCID
      href: https://orcid.org/0009-0000-6673-3838
    - icon: brands zhihu
      text: 知乎
      href: https://www.zhihu.com/people/arlionn/
    - icon: brands weibo
      text: 微博
      href: https://weibo.com/arlionn
    - icon: brands xing
      text: 小红书
      href: https://www.xiaohongshu.com/user/profile/5e5fb0a70000000001007a88?xsec_token=AB3GLf7TV9coqTZ78EIzCEQmd9ubKKUJRaP2d9uN2umB8%3D&xsec_source=pc_search
    - icon: 📝
      text: CV
      href: CV.pdf  
---

::: {#hero-heading}

Dr Bai's research interests lie in the field of underground hydrogen storage, with specific applications in salt cavern. He has been actively researching topics such as renewable energy,energy transition, rock mechanics, and geomechanics. 





## Background

- 2026-Present: Ph.D in Civil Engineering,University of Chinese Academy of Sciences, China, specializing in Underground Hydrogen Storage.
- 2022-2025: Ms.C. in Civil and Hydraulic ,University of Chinese Academy of Sciences, China, specializing in Underground Hydrogen Storage.
- 2017-2021: Bachelor of Engineering, Shandong University of Science and Technology, China, majoring in Civil Engineering.

:::
